glorify.en: praise someone or something highly, especially in a religious or reverent way

ex.en: The song glorifies the courage of soldiers.

glorify.en: make something seem more attractive, exciting, or acceptable than it really is

ex.en: The movie has been criticized for glorifying violence.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 폭력을 미화한다는 비판을 받았다.

ex.en: The candle gave off a warm glow.

glow.en: shine with a soft, steady light, without flames

ex.en: The embers continued to glow in the dark.

glow.en: have a warm, bright color in your face, especially from health, heat, or emotion

ex.en: Her cheeks glowed after the run.
ex.ko: 그녀는 달리기를 하고 나서 뺨이 발그레해졌다.

glow.en: a strong feeling of pleasure, satisfaction, or pride

ex.en: She felt a glow of pride after winning the award.
ex.ko: 그녀는 상을 받고 나서 벅찬 자부심을 느꼈다.

goat.en: a farm animal with horns and a beard, related to sheep

ex.en: The goat ate grass in the field.

goat.en: a person who is blamed or made fun of by others

ex.en: He became the goat of the team after missing the goal.
ex.ko: 그는 골을 놓친 후 팀의 웃음거리가 되었다.

goat.en: (informal, often written GOAT) the greatest player or person of all time

ex.en: Many fans consider him the goat of basketball.
ex.ko: 많은 팬들은 그를 농구 역대 최고 선수로 여긴다.

god.en: the being who is worshipped as the creator and ruler of the universe, especially in Christianity, Judaism, and Islam

ex.en: Many people pray to God every day.

god.en: a being who is believed to control some part of nature or life, worshipped in a religion

ex.en: The ancient Greeks worshipped many gods.

god.en: a person admired so much that they seem almost divine

ex.en: To his fans, the singer is a god.
ex.ko: 팬들에게 그 가수는 신 같은 존재다.

godly.en: showing deep respect and love for God

ex.en: She led a godly life devoted to prayer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기도에 헌신하는 경건한 삶을 살았다.

goodwill.en: a friendly, helpful attitude toward other people

ex.en: The charity event was held in a spirit of goodwill.
ex.ko: 그 자선 행사는 선의의 정신으로 열렸다.

goodwill.en: (business) the good reputation of a company, counted as a financial asset

ex.en: The company paid extra for the brand's goodwill.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 브랜드의 영업권에 대해 추가 비용을 지불했다.

gorge.en: a deep, narrow valley with steep, rocky sides

ex.en: They hiked along the edge of the gorge.

gorge.en: eat a large amount of food greedily

ex.en: The kids gorged themselves on candy at the party.

governmental.en: relating to the government of a country or state

ex.en: The reform requires governmental approval.

gracefulness.en: the quality of moving or behaving in a smooth, attractive way

ex.en: The dancer moved with great gracefulness.

gradual.en: happening slowly or in small steps over a period of time

ex.en: There has been a gradual improvement in her health.

grain.en: the small, hard seeds of food plants such as wheat, rice, or corn

ex.en: The farmer stored the grain in a large silo.

grain.en: a small, hard piece of a substance such as sand, salt, or sugar

ex.en: A grain of sand got into her shoe.
ex.ko: 모래 한 알갱이가 그녀의 신발에 들어갔다.

grain.en: the natural pattern of lines in wood, stone, or fabric

ex.en: The table was polished to show off the wood's grain.
ex.ko: 그 탁자는 나뭇결이 드러나게 광을 냈다.

grand.en: large, impressive, and grand in size or style

ex.en: They live in a grand old house.
We had a grand time at the festival.
ex.ko: 우리는 축제에서 정말 즐거운 시간을 보냈다.

ex.en: The car cost him ten grand.
ex.ko: 그 차는 그에게 만 달러가 들었다.

graphically.en: in a way that is very clear and detailed, sometimes shockingly so

ex.en: The book describes the battle graphically.

graphically.en: in the form of a diagram, chart, or graph

ex.en: The results are shown graphically in the report.

grasp.en: understand something fully, especially a difficult idea

ex.en: It took him a while to grasp the concept.
ex.ko: 그는 그 개념을 파악하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

ex.en: She has a good grasp of English grammar.

grasp.en: take hold of something firmly with your hand

ex.en: She grasped the rope tightly.

gravestone.en: a stone placed over a grave with the dead person's name on it

ex.en: They placed flowers by the gravestone.

gravitation.en: the natural force that attracts objects toward each other, especially toward the earth

ex.en: Gravitation keeps the planets in orbit around the sun.
ex.ko: 중력은 행성들을 태양 궤도에 붙잡아 둔다.

gravitation.en: a natural tendency to move toward or be attracted to something

ex.en: There has been a gravitation toward remote work in recent years.
ex.ko: 최근 몇 년간 재택근무로의 쏠림 현상이 있었다.

greatness.en: the quality of being very important, successful, or admired

ex.en: He achieved greatness as a scientist.

greatness.en: very large size, extent, or number

ex.en: The greatness of the universe is hard to imagine.

grief.en: a feeling of deep sadness, especially after someone dies

ex.en: She was overcome with grief after her father's death.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아버지가 돌아가신 후 비탄에 잠겼다.

grieve.en: feel or show deep sadness, especially because someone has died

ex.en: He is still grieving for his wife.
The doctor gave a grim look after the exam.
ex.ko: 의사는 검사 후 침울한 표정을 지었다.

grim.en: very serious and determined, without any softness

ex.en: She fought with grim determination.
The movie had some grim scenes of violence.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 섬뜩한 폭력 장면이 있었다.

ex.en: She had a big grin on her face.
He grinned when he saw the surprise party.
ex.ko: 그는 깜짝 파티를 보고 활짝 웃었다.

ex.en: He gripped the steering wheel tightly.

grip.en: the part of an object designed to be held

ex.en: The tennis racket has a rubber grip.
ex.ko: 그 테니스 라켓은 고무 손잡이가 있다.

grip.en: hold someone's attention or emotions completely

ex.en: The movie gripped the audience from the start.

grip.en: the ability of tires, shoes, etc. to hold firmly onto a surface without slipping

ex.en: These boots have good grip on ice.
ex.ko: 이 부츠는 얼음 위에서 접지력이 좋다.

gross.en: (of an amount of money) total, before tax or other costs are taken away

ex.en: Her gross salary is $50,000 a year.
That smell is so gross.

gross.en: earn a total amount of money before tax and expenses are subtracted

ex.en: The film grossed over $100 million.

growing.en: becoming larger, greater, or more in amount

ex.en: There is growing concern about climate change.
ex.ko: 기후 변화에 대한 우려가 커지고 있다.

ex.en: The grown-ups talked while the children played.
ex.ko: 아이들이 노는 동안 어른들은 이야기를 나눴다.

grown-up.en: physically or mentally mature, like an adult

ex.en: She has a very grown-up attitude for her age.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나이에 비해 아주 성숙한 태도를 가지고 있다.

guerrilla.en: a member of a small, irregular fighting force that attacks a larger, official army

ex.en: The guerrillas hid in the mountains.

guideline.en: a rule or piece of advice about how to do something correctly

ex.en: Please follow the safety guidelines.

gulp.en: a large amount of liquid swallowed at one time

ex.en: He took a big gulp of water.
ex.ko: 그는 물을 한 모금 크게 마셨다.

gulp.en: swallow something quickly in large amounts

ex.en: He gulped down his coffee before running out the door.
ex.ko: 그는 문을 나서기 전에 커피를 벌컥벌컥 마셨다.

gulp.en: swallow nervously, especially because you are afraid or anxious

ex.en: She gulped nervously before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전에 긴장해서 침을 꼴깍 삼켰다.

gum.en: a soft, sweet substance that you chew but do not swallow

ex.en: She chewed a piece of gum in class.

gum.en: the firm pink flesh in the mouth that surrounds the base of the teeth

ex.en: His gums were bleeding after the dentist visit.
ex.ko: 치과 방문 후 그의 잇몸에서 피가 났다.

gunshot.en: the sound of a gun being fired

ex.en: They heard a gunshot in the distance.

gunshot.en: a wound caused by a bullet

ex.en: He was treated for a gunshot wound.

guts.en: courage and determination to do something difficult or risky

ex.en: It takes guts to quit a stable job.

guts.en: the organs inside the stomach and body

ex.en: The fish's guts were removed before cooking.

gymnastic.en: relating to gymnastics or exercises that develop strength and agility

ex.en: The gymnastic routine impressed the judges.

habitant.en: a person who lives in a particular place

ex.en: The habitants of the island rely on fishing.

hail.en: small balls of ice that fall like rain during a storm

ex.en: Hail damaged the crops last night.

hail.en: call out to stop a taxi or other vehicle

ex.en: She hailed a taxi outside the hotel.
ex.ko: 그녀는 호텔 밖에서 택시를 불러 세웠다.

hail.en: praise someone or something publicly and enthusiastically

ex.en: The scientist was hailed as a genius.

hail.en: (hail from) come originally from a particular place

ex.en: She hails from a small town in Ireland.

hairy.en: covered with a lot of hair

ex.en: The dog has a hairy coat.
That was a hairy drive down the mountain.

handicap.en: something that makes it harder for someone to succeed or make progress

ex.en: Not speaking the language was a big handicap for him.
ex.ko: 그 언어를 못하는 것은 그에게 큰 불리한 조건이었다.

handicap.cue: 골프 등 경기에서 실력 차 보정
handicap.en: an advantage given to a weaker player to make a competition fairer

ex.en: His golf handicap is twelve.

handicap.en: (somewhat old-fashioned) a physical or mental condition that limits a person's abilities

ex.en: The building was designed for people with a handicap.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 장애가 있는 사람들을 위해 설계되었다.

handicraft.en: skilled work done by hand, such as sewing or pottery, or an object made this way

ex.en: The market sells local handicrafts.

handout.en: a printed sheet of information given out in a class or meeting

ex.en: The teacher gave out a handout before the lecture.

handout.en: money, food, or clothing given free to someone in need

ex.en: The family relies on government handouts.

handrail.en: a rail that you hold onto for support on stairs or a balcony

ex.en: Hold the handrail while going down the stairs.

hard drive.en: the part of a computer that stores data permanently

ex.en: I need to back up my files on an external hard drive.
ex.ko: 외장 하드 드라이브에 파일을 백업해야 한다.

hardware.en: the physical parts of a computer system, as opposed to software

ex.en: The company sells both computer hardware and software.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 컴퓨터 하드웨어와 소프트웨어를 모두 판매한다.

hardware.en: small metal tools and items such as nails, screws, and locks

ex.en: He bought some hardware at the store to fix the fence.
ex.ko: 그는 울타리를 고치기 위해 가게에서 철물을 좀 샀다.

hardware.en: military equipment such as weapons, tanks, or vehicles

ex.en: The country invested heavily in military hardware.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 군사 장비에 막대한 투자를 했다.

harmless.en: not able or likely to cause harm or damage

ex.en: The spider looks scary but is harmless.

harrow.en: a farming tool with metal teeth used to break up and smooth soil

ex.en: The farmer pulled the harrow across the field.

harrow.en: break up and smooth soil using a harrow

ex.en: He harrowed the field before planting seeds.
ex.ko: 그는 씨를 뿌리기 전에 밭을 써레질했다.

haste.en: great speed in doing something, often causing mistakes

ex.en: In his haste, he forgot his keys.

hastily.en: in a hurried way, often without enough care

ex.en: She hastily packed her bags and left.

hatching.en: the process of young animals coming out of eggs

ex.en: The hatching of the chicks took three weeks.

hatching.en: a drawing technique using crossing lines to show shading

ex.en: The artist used hatching to create shadow in the sketch.
ex.ko: 화가는 스케치에 그림자를 나타내려고 해칭 기법을 사용했다.

haunting.en: so beautiful, sad, or disturbing that it stays in your mind

ex.en: The song has a haunting melody.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 뇌리에서 떠나지 않는 멜로디를 가지고 있다.

hay.en: grass that has been cut and dried to feed farm animals

ex.en: The farmer stored hay in the barn for winter.
ex.ko: 농부는 겨울을 위해 헛간에 건초를 저장했다.

hazardous.en: involving danger or risk, especially to health or safety

ex.en: The factory handles hazardous chemicals.

headquarters.en: the main office of a company or organization

ex.en: The company's headquarters is in Seoul.

headquarters.en: the place from which a military operation is commanded

ex.en: The general returned to headquarters after the battle.

health care.en: services provided to maintain or improve people's health

ex.en: Everyone should have access to good health care.
ex.ko: 모든 사람은 좋은 의료 서비스를 이용할 수 있어야 한다.

heap.en: a pile of things put on top of each other

ex.en: There was a heap of clothes on the floor.
She heaped the dishes in the sink.

heap.en: give someone a large amount of praise, criticism, etc.

ex.en: Critics heaped praise on the new film.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 새 영화에 칭찬을 잔뜩 퍼부었다.

ex.en: It was heartbreaking to see the flooded homes.
ex.ko: 물에 잠긴 집들을 보는 것은 가슴 아팠다.

heartwarming.en: making you feel happy and emotionally moved

ex.en: The reunion was a heartwarming moment.

hedge.en: a row of bushes or small trees planted close together as a fence or boundary

ex.en: They planted a hedge around the garden.

hedge.en: a way of protecting yourself against financial loss

ex.en: Gold is often seen as a hedge against inflation.
ex.ko: 금은 흔히 인플레이션에 대한 헤지 수단으로 여겨진다.

hedge.en: avoid giving a clear or direct answer

ex.en: He hedged when asked about his plans.

helicopter.en: an aircraft that flies using rotating blades on top

ex.en: A helicopter landed on the roof.

hell.en: (in religion) the place where evil people are believed to be punished after death

ex.en: In many religions, sinners are said to go to hell.
ex.ko: 많은 종교에서 죄인은 지옥에 간다고 한다.

hell.ko: (비유) 아주 끔찍한 상황, 지옥 같은 곳
hell.en: (informal) a very unpleasant or difficult experience or situation

ex.en: The last few weeks at work have been hell.
ex.ko: 지난 몇 주간의 직장 생활은 지옥 같았다.

helper.en: a person who helps another person or organization

ex.en: She works as a helper at the local shelter.

hemp.en: a plant fiber used to make rope, cloth, and paper

ex.en: The bag was made of hemp fabric.

hemp.en: the cannabis plant, sometimes grown for fiber or as a drug

ex.en: Hemp is grown in some countries for industrial use.

herb.en: a plant used to add flavor to food or for medicine

ex.en: She grows fresh herbs like basil and mint in her garden.
ex.ko: 그녀는 정원에서 바질과 민트 같은 신선한 허브를 기른다.

heritage.en: traditions, achievements, and beliefs that are part of a group's history

ex.en: The festival celebrates the region's cultural heritage.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 그 지역의 문화유산을 기념한다.

hesitation.en: a pause before doing or saying something because you are uncertain

ex.en: She agreed without hesitation.

high-tech.en: using the most modern methods and technology

ex.en: The factory uses high-tech robots.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 첨단 기술 로봇을 사용한다.

hijack.en: illegally seize control of a vehicle, especially an airplane, often using force

ex.en: Terrorists hijacked the plane.

hijack.en: take control of something, such as a meeting or plan, and use it for your own purposes

ex.en: The discussion was hijacked by a small group with their own agenda.
ex.ko: 그 토론은 자기 의도를 가진 소수 그룹에게 가로채졌다.

ex.en: The comedian's jokes were hilarious.

hint.en: an indirect suggestion that helps you understand or guess something

ex.en: She gave me a hint about the surprise party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 깜짝 파티에 대한 힌트를 나에게 줬다.

ex.en: He hinted that he might quit his job.
ex.ko: 그는 회사를 그만둘지도 모른다고 넌지시 말했다.

hint.en: a very small amount of something, often barely noticeable

ex.en: There was a hint of sadness in her voice.
ex.ko: 그녀의 목소리에는 약간의 슬픔이 배어 있었다.

holder.en: a person who owns or possesses something, such as a ticket or title

ex.en: Ticket holders can enter through this gate.
ex.ko: 티켓 소유자는 이 문으로 입장할 수 있다.

holder.en: a device that holds or supports something

ex.en: She put her phone in the holder on the dashboard.
The tree trunk was hollow.
ex.ko: 그 나무 줄기는 속이 비어 있었다.

hollow.en: having no real meaning, value, or sincerity

ex.en: His apology sounded hollow.

hollow.en: remove the inside part of something

ex.en: They hollowed out the pumpkin to make a lantern.
ex.ko: 그들은 랜턴을 만들려고 호박 속을 파냈다.

hollow.en: a hole or empty space in something

ex.en: Water collected in a hollow in the rock.
ex.ko: 바위의 움푹 팬 곳에 물이 고였다.

holocaust.en: a situation in which a great many people are killed and there is great destruction, especially in war

ex.en: The bombing caused a holocaust in the city.
ex.ko: 그 폭격은 그 도시에 대참사를 일으켰다.

holocaust.en: (the Holocaust) the mass murder of Jewish people by the Nazis during World War II

ex.en: The museum tells the history of the Holocaust.

homeward.en: moving toward or leading to home

ex.en: They began their homeward journey at sunset.
ex.ko: 그들은 해질 무렵 집으로 향하는 여정을 시작했다.

hone.en: improve a skill over time through practice

ex.en: She honed her writing skills over many years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오랜 세월에 걸쳐 글쓰기 실력을 갈고닦았다.

honorable.en: deserving respect because of good, honest, and moral behavior

ex.en: He made an honorable decision to admit his mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 실수를 인정하는 명예로운 결정을 내렸다.

hood.en: a covering for the head that is attached to a piece of clothing

ex.en: She pulled up her hood to keep warm.

hood.en: (American English) the metal cover over a car's engine

ex.en: He opened the hood to check the engine.

hood.en: (informal, especially American slang) the neighborhood where someone lives

ex.en: He grew up in a rough hood.

hook.en: a curved piece of metal or plastic used for hanging or catching things

ex.en: She hung her coat on the hook.

hook.en: attach or catch something using a hook

ex.en: He hooked the trailer to the truck.

hook.en: a short punch thrown with a bent arm, swung from the side

ex.en: The boxer landed a strong right hook.
ex.ko: 그 복서는 강한 라이트 훅을 명중시켰다.

hook.en: something that attracts people's interest or attention

ex.en: The song has a catchy hook that people remember.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 사람들이 기억하는 확 끄는 후렴구가 있다.

hospice.en: a place or program that provides care for people who are dying

ex.en: She spent her final weeks in a hospice.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마지막 몇 주를 호스피스에서 보냈다.

hospitable.en: friendly and welcoming to guests or visitors

ex.en: The locals were very hospitable to tourists.

hospitable.en: (of a place or environment) suitable and favorable for living or growing

ex.en: The desert is not hospitable to most plants.
ex.ko: 사막은 대부분의 식물에게 살기 적합하지 않다.

hospitality.en: friendly and generous treatment of guests or visitors

ex.en: Thank you for your warm hospitality.

hospitalize.en: admit someone to a hospital for treatment

ex.en: He was hospitalized after the accident.

hourly.en: happening every hour, or calculated by the hour

ex.en: She earns an hourly wage of $15.
Buses run hourly from the station.

housekeeper.en: a person employed to manage or clean a household

ex.en: The hotel's housekeeper cleaned the rooms every day.
ex.ko: 그 호텔의 가정부는 매일 객실을 청소했다.

housemaster.en: (British English) a teacher in charge of a boarding house at a school

ex.en: The housemaster checked on the students every night.
ex.ko: 그 사감은 매일 밤 학생들을 확인했다.

housewife.en: a woman who manages her household while her husband earns income

ex.en: She has been a housewife for ten years.

hover.en: stay in one place in the air without moving up or down

ex.en: The helicopter hovered above the building.

hover.en: stay close to a place or person, often anxiously or uncertainly

ex.en: She hovered near the door, unsure whether to knock.
ex.ko: 그녀는 노크할지 망설이며 문 근처를 서성였다.

ex.en: The movie was hugely successful.

human rights.en: the basic rights that every person should have, such as freedom and equality

ex.en: The organization fights for human rights around the world.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 전 세계 인권을 위해 싸운다.

humane.en: showing kindness and concern for the suffering of others

ex.en: The shelter provides humane treatment for animals.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 동물에게 인도적인 대우를 제공한다.

humanist.en: a person who believes in human reason, values, and dignity rather than religious belief

ex.en: He was a humanist who valued reason over dogma.

humanize.en: make something or someone more humane, kind, or relatable

ex.en: The film humanizes the struggles of refugees.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 난민들의 고충에 인간미를 더한다.

humble.en: not proud or arrogant; modest about your abilities

ex.en: Despite his success, he remained humble.

humble.en: make someone feel less proud or important, often by showing they were wrong

ex.en: The defeat humbled the champion.
ex.ko: 그 패배는 챔피언의 기를 꺾어 놓았다.

humble.en: simple and not special or grand, especially in origin

ex.en: He came from humble beginnings.

humbug.en: nonsense or foolish talk meant to trick people

ex.en: "Humbug!" he shouted, dismissing the whole idea.
ex.ko: "허튼소리!" 그는 그 생각 전체를 일축하며 소리쳤다.

humbug.en: a dishonest scheme intended to deceive people

ex.en: The investment scheme turned out to be a humbug.

humiliate.en: make someone feel ashamed or foolish in front of others

ex.en: He was humiliated in front of his classmates.
ex.ko: 그는 반 친구들 앞에서 굴욕을 당했다.

humiliating.en: making you feel ashamed or foolish

ex.en: Losing the match in front of everyone was humiliating.
ex.ko: 모두가 보는 앞에서 경기에 진 것은 굴욕적이었다.

humiliation.en: the feeling of being made to look foolish or losing respect

ex.en: She felt the humiliation of being fired in front of her team.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팀원들 앞에서 해고당하는 굴욕을 느꼈다.

hunting.en: the activity of chasing and killing wild animals for food or sport

ex.en: Hunting is banned in this national park.

hunting.en: the activity of searching thoroughly for something

ex.en: He spent months house hunting before buying.
ex.ko: 그는 집을 사기 전 몇 달 동안 집을 찾아다녔다.

hush.en: make someone stop talking or making noise

ex.en: She hushed the crying baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우는 아기를 달래 조용히 시켰다.

hush.en: a sudden silence, especially in a place that was noisy

ex.en: A hush fell over the room.

hybrid.en: made by combining two different things or types

ex.en: The car uses a hybrid engine that runs on gas and electricity.
ex.ko: 그 차는 휘발유와 전기로 가는 하이브리드 엔진을 사용한다.

hybrid.en: an animal or plant produced from two different species or varieties

ex.en: This flower is a hybrid of two rose varieties.
ex.ko: 이 꽃은 두 장미 품종의 교배종이다.

hygiene.en: practices that keep you and your surroundings clean and healthy

ex.en: Good hygiene helps prevent the spread of disease.
ex.ko: 좋은 위생 습관은 질병 확산을 막는 데 도움이 된다.

hyperbole.en: language that exaggerates something for effect, not meant to be taken literally

ex.en: "I've told you a million times" is an example of hyperbole.

hyphen.en: a short line (-) used to join words or split a word at the end of a line

ex.en: "Well-known" is written with a hyphen.

hyphen.en: join words or syllables using a hyphen

ex.en: You should hyphenate compound adjectives like "well-known".
ex.ko: "well-known" 같은 복합 형용사는 하이픈으로 연결해야 한다.

iceman.en: (old-fashioned) a person whose job was to deliver ice, before refrigerators were common

ex.en: The iceman used to visit houses every week.
ex.ko: 얼음 배달원은 예전에 매주 집집마다 방문하곤 했다.

icon.en: a small picture on a computer screen that represents a program or file

ex.en: Click the icon to open the app.

icon.en: a person or thing that represents an idea or is very famous and admired

ex.en: She became a fashion icon in the 1990s.

icy.en: extremely cold, or covered with ice

ex.en: The roads were icy this morning.

icy.en: (of a person's manner) unfriendly and cold

ex.en: She gave him an icy stare.

ideally.en: in a perfect situation, though this may not be realistic

ex.en: Ideally, we should leave before rush hour.

identical.en: exactly the same in every way

ex.en: The twins wore identical outfits.

identical.en: (of twins) developed from a single fertilized egg

ex.en: They are identical twins, not fraternal.

identification.en: an official document that proves who you are

ex.en: Please show your identification at the entrance.

identification.en: the act of recognizing or naming who or what someone or something is

ex.en: The identification of the suspect took several days.
ex.ko: 그 용의자의 신원 확인에는 며칠이 걸렸다.

identify.en: recognize and name who or what someone or something is

ex.en: Police identified the suspect from the video.

identify.en: feel that you share the same feelings or experiences as someone

ex.en: Many readers identify with the main character.

ideology.en: a set of beliefs or ideas that guides a person, group, or political system

ex.en: The two parties have very different political ideologies.
ex.ko: 그 두 정당은 매우 다른 정치 이념을 가지고 있다.

idiot.en: (informal) a stupid person, or someone who has done something foolish

ex.en: I felt like an idiot for forgetting the tickets.
He spent an idle afternoon watching TV.
ex.ko: 그는 게으르게 오후 내내 TV를 보며 보냈다.

idle.en: (of an engine) run slowly while not doing any work

ex.en: The car idled outside the store.
ex.ko: 그 차는 가게 밖에서 공회전을 하고 있었다.

idolize.en: admire or love someone so much that you see them as perfect

ex.en: Young fans idolize the pop star.

ignorance.en: a lack of knowledge or information about something

ex.en: His mistake was due to ignorance, not malice.
ex.ko: 그의 실수는 악의가 아니라 무지 때문이었다.

ignorant.en: lacking knowledge or information about something

ex.en: She was ignorant of the new rules.

ignorant.en: lacking education or knowledge in general

ex.en: He was mocked for his ignorant remarks.

illustrate.en: explain or make something clear by giving examples

ex.en: The teacher illustrated the point with a real-life story.

illustrate.en: add pictures to a book or explain something with pictures

ex.en: The children's book is illustrated with colorful drawings.
ex.ko: 그 동화책은 화려한 그림으로 삽화가 그려져 있다.

illustration.en: a picture in a book, magazine, or document

ex.en: The book is full of beautiful illustrations.

illustration.en: an example that helps explain or clarify something

ex.en: As an illustration of the problem, consider this case.
ex.ko: 그 문제의 예시로 이 사례를 생각해 보자.

imagery.en: descriptive language that creates pictures in the reader's mind

ex.en: The poem is full of vivid imagery of the sea.
ex.ko: 그 시는 바다의 생생한 심상으로 가득하다.

imitation.en: something made to look like the real thing, but not genuine

ex.en: The bag is an imitation, not the real designer brand.
ex.ko: 그 가방은 진짜 명품이 아니라 모조품이다.

imitation.en: the act of copying someone's actions, speech, or style

ex.en: Children learn language partly through imitation.
ex.ko: 아이들은 부분적으로 모방을 통해 언어를 배운다.

immerse.en: put something completely into a liquid

ex.en: She immersed the vegetables in cold water.

immerse.en: involve yourself completely in an activity

ex.en: He immersed himself in his studies.

immigrant.en: a person who comes to live permanently in a country that is not their own

ex.en: Many immigrants come to the city for work.
ex.ko: 많은 이민자들이 일자리를 찾아 그 도시로 온다.

immoral.en: not following accepted standards of right and wrong behavior

ex.en: Lying to your business partners is immoral.

immortal.en: living or lasting forever; not able to die

ex.en: In the myth, the gods are immortal.

immortal.en: a person, such as a writer or artist, who will be remembered forever

ex.en: Shakespeare is considered one of literature's immortals.
ex.ko: 셰익스피어는 문학의 불후의 인물 중 하나로 여겨진다.

immortality.en: the state of living or existing forever

ex.en: Many myths explore humans' desire for immortality.
ex.ko: 많은 신화가 불멸을 향한 인간의 욕망을 다룬다.

impartial.en: not favoring one side more than another; fair

ex.en: The judge must remain impartial.

impatiently.en: in a way that shows you cannot wait calmly

ex.en: She tapped her foot impatiently while waiting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기다리는 동안 초조하게 발을 굴렀다.

imperative.en: extremely important and urgent; something that must be done

ex.en: It is imperative that we act now.

imperative.en: (grammar) the verb form used to give orders, such as "Stop!"

ex.en: "Sit down" is an example of the imperative.

impermanent.en: lasting only for a short or limited time

ex.en: The exhibit is an impermanent display of modern art.
ex.ko: 그 전시는 현대 미술의 일시적인 전시다.

implement.en: put a plan, decision, or system into effect

ex.en: The company will implement the new policy next month.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 다음 달에 새 정책을 시행할 것이다.

implement.en: a tool used to do a particular job

ex.en: Farmers use various implements to plow the field.
ex.ko: 농부들은 밭을 갈기 위해 다양한 도구를 사용한다.

implication.cue: 직접 말하지 않아도 알 수 있는 의미
implication.en: a meaning that is suggested but not directly stated

ex.en: His silence carried an implication of guilt.
ex.ko: 그의 침묵에는 죄책감의 함의가 담겨 있었다.

implication.en: a possible effect or result of a decision or action, often used in plural

ex.en: We need to consider the implications of this policy.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 정책의 영향을 고려해야 한다.

imply.en: suggest something without saying it directly

ex.en: Her tone implied that she disagreed.
It's impolite to interrupt someone while they're talking.

impolitely.en: in a rude or bad-mannered way

ex.en: He spoke impolitely to the waiter.

import.en: bring goods or services into a country from abroad

ex.en: The country imports most of its oil.

import.en: a product brought into a country from abroad

ex.en: Japanese cars are popular imports in many countries.
ex.ko: 일본 자동차는 많은 나라에서 인기 있는 수입품이다.

import.en: (computing) bring data into a program or document from another file

ex.en: You can import the spreadsheet into the database.
ex.ko: 그 스프레드시트를 데이터베이스로 불러올 수 있다.

impose.en: officially force a rule, tax, or punishment to be obeyed or paid

ex.en: The government imposed a new tax on imports.

impose.en: force your ideas, beliefs, or wishes onto someone else

ex.en: She refused to impose her opinions on others.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 의견을 남에게 강요하기를 거부했다.

impossibility.en: the fact of something being unable to happen or exist

ex.en: They discussed the impossibility of finishing on time.
ex.ko: 그들은 제시간에 끝내는 것이 불가능하다는 점을 논의했다.

impractical.en: not sensible or realistic to do in practice

ex.en: The plan sounded good but was impractical.

impressed.en: feeling admiration because of something impressive

ex.en: I was impressed by her presentation.

impressionism.cue: 빛과 순간의 인상을 그리는 미술 사조
impressionism.en: a 19th-century art movement that captured light and momentary impressions using loose brushstrokes

ex.en: Monet is one of the founders of Impressionism.
ex.ko: 모네는 인상주의의 창시자 중 한 명이다.

impressionist.en: a painter who works in the style of Impressionism

ex.en: The gallery displayed works by famous Impressionists.
ex.ko: 그 미술관은 유명한 인상주의 화가들의 작품을 전시했다.

imprison.en: put someone in prison or lock them up

ex.en: He was imprisoned for three years for fraud.

imprisonment.en: the state of being kept in prison, or the punishment of being put there

ex.en: The crime carries a sentence of five years' imprisonment.

improbable.ko: 있을 법하지 않은, 사실 같지 않은
improbable.en: not likely to be true or to happen

ex.en: It seems improbable that they will finish by tomorrow.
ex.ko: 그들이 내일까지 끝낼 것 같지는 않다.

improperly.en: in a way that is not correct, suitable, or appropriate

ex.en: The equipment was improperly installed.

improved.en: made better in quality or condition

ex.en: The new model has improved battery life.

impulse.en: a sudden strong wish to do something, without thinking about the results

ex.en: She bought the dress on impulse.

impulse.en: an electrical signal that travels along a nerve

ex.en: Nerve impulses travel from the brain to the muscles.

impulsive.en: acting suddenly without thinking carefully first

ex.en: He made an impulsive decision to quit his job.
The report contained several inaccurate figures.
ex.ko: 그 보고서에는 부정확한 수치가 여러 개 있었다.

inadequate.en: not enough or not good enough for what is needed

ex.en: The funding was inadequate to complete the project.
A rock is an inanimate object.

inborn.en: existing in a person from birth; natural

ex.en: She has an inborn talent for music.

incense.en: a substance that produces a pleasant smell when burned

ex.en: She lit incense to fill the room with fragrance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 방을 향기로 채우려고 향을 피웠다.

ex.en: The unfair decision incensed the workers.

incentive.en: something that encourages you to do something

ex.en: The company offers incentives to attract new employees.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 신입 직원을 유치하기 위해 유인책을 제공한다.

incentive.en: extra money given to employees as a reward for good performance

ex.en: Salespeople receive a cash incentive for meeting targets.
ex.ko: 영업 사원들은 목표를 달성하면 현금 인센티브를 받는다.
